package main import ( "fmt" "html" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail constructs an HTML-formatted email body for user feedback // that can be sent to the customer support team using net/smtp. // The function takes the sender address, recipient address, username, // feedback subject, and feedback content as input parameters. // It returns the complete email body as a string and an error if any. func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { // Escape HTML special characters to prevent XSS and ensure proper formatting safeUserName := html.EscapeString(userName) safeSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) safeFeedbackContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Construct the email headers headers := make(map[string]string) headers["From"] = fromAddr headers["To"] = toAddr headers["Subject"] = subject headers["MIME-Version"] = "1.0" headers["Content-Type"] = "text/html; charset=\"utf-8\"" // Build the header section headerSection := "" for key, value := range headers { headerSection += fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s\r\n", key, value) } // Build the HTML body content htmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(` Feedback Received

New User Feedback Received

Username: %s

Subject: %s

Feedback Content:

%s

This feedback was automatically generated by the user feedback system.

`, safeUserName, safeSubject, safeFeedbackContent) // Combine headers and body with proper SMTP format emailBody = headerSection + "\r\n" + htmlBody return emailBody, nil }
